root_motivation: Prevent warehouse credit waste by proactively routing a BoundMqo to the cheapest capable backend before execution — DAX for small aggregates, MDX for multidimensional shape, SQL/PGWire-streaming for large extracts — using the MQO's grain-based pre-execution row estimate, which is only computable because the MQO is structured.
user_persona: AtScale MQO fleet engineers and the mcp-server runtime, which call `mqo-route --bound <file> --stats <file>` to get a JSON routing decision before dispatching a query to the backend engine.

unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --release 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{sum += $4} END {print sum}'

acceptance_criteria.description: An aggregated MQO over low-cardinality levels routes to `dax`.
An MQO flagged drill-through, asymmetric-axes, or cellset-requested routes to `mdx`.
An MQO whose estimated rows exceed the threshold routes to `sql` and emits a non-empty `sql_projection`.
`estimated_rows` is computed as the product of selected level cardinalities, reduced by equality filters; the formula is asserted in a unit test.
`--row-threshold` overrides the default (50,000) and changes the routing boundary.
`cargo test --release` passes; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` is clean.
acceptance_criteria.test: CI: cargo test --release && cargo clippy -- -D warnings exits 0

acceptance_criteria.description: The binary `mqo-route` can be invoked end-to-end via integration test with real JSON fixture files.

scope: Rust CLI binary `mqo-route` that reads --bound <BoundMqo JSON> and --stats <StatBundle JSON>
StatBundle carrying level_cardinalities (HashMap<String,u64>) and ShapeFlags (asymmetric_axes, drill_through, cellset_requested)
Row estimation: product of level cardinalities, filtered by Member filters
Backend routing: MDX-shape-first, then SQL-if-over-threshold, then DAX default
Flat SQL SELECT projection emitted for sql backend
JSON routing decision on stdout: { backend, estimated_rows, reason, sql_projection? }
non_goals: No DAX, MDX, or SQL query compilation — projection is intentionally stub-level
No query execution or network calls
No caching or memoisation of cardinality estimates
No dynamic model introspection — stats are always caller-supplied
No cost-based optimizer; row threshold is the only routing signal beyond shape flags

additional.note: mqo-catalog-binder listed in Cargo.toml but unused in lib — remove or keep as optional future dep

five_whys_trace.q: Why route to different backends at all — why not always use DAX?
five_whys_trace.a: DAX trips the engine's row cap on large extracts, spending warehouse credits and returning zero rows.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must the routing be pre-execution rather than post-MDX-overflow?
five_whys_trace.a: By the time MDX overflows, the warehouse credits are already consumed. The 2026-02-18 engine blog confirms PGWire streaming makes large tabular pulls heap-safe, so SQL is the correct escape hatch — but only if chosen before the MDX call is made.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is a pre-execution estimate even possible?
five_whys_trace.a: The MQO is a structured object with a declared grain (selected levels), so the result cardinality is the product of level cardinalities — computable from cached stats before touching the engine.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does this belong in a separate CLI rather than inside mcp-server?
five_whys_trace.a: Separation lets the routing decision be testable independently, invokable from the orchestrator tier, and replaceable without redeploying the server. The router is also the right seam for future cost-model upgrades (e.g. per-level null rate).

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does mqo-catalog-binder need to be a runtime dependency?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No — the PRD says 'consumes BoundMqo JSON + catalog stats'; both arrive as JSON files. The binder is the producer, not a linked dep. Remove mqo-catalog-binder from Cargo.toml; the compiler warning confirms it has no lib target.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is a SHOULD-level integration CLI test required for the risk gate?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes — SKILL.md §'Binary integration test gap (mqo-spec, 2026-06-08)' adds tests/integration_cli.rs to the scaffold for [[bin]] projects. It catches missed mutants in binary dispatch arms.
